_comment: Per-persona expected deliverable patterns. Used by verify-deliverables.sh (SubagentStop hook). Keyed by the dispatchable personas. required_markers lists the full 6-marker vocabulary — at least one must appear as an H2. Enforced fields: required_markers, forbidden_paths, must_not_modify, must_not_run_bash, required_verification; expected_paths and should_* fields are advisory. forge/pipeline/quill are tombstone fallbacks for pre-split dispatches. A returning persona with NO entry here is NOT silently passed — verify-deliverables.sh emits a WARN.

codex-worker._note: INERT entry on a standard package install: broker.registry registers codex-worker ONLY when .claude/agents/codex-worker.md exists on the running machine (this-machine contrib-lane feature, never shipped in nexus-package/.claude/agents/) — see registry.py's _codex_lane_agent_files_present. This row exists only because test_deliverables_persona_drift.py asserts every DISPATCHABLE_PERSONAS member resolves here on a codex-lane-enabled machine. A RELAY persona: work happens inside the Codex CLI subprocess, not via this agent's own Edit/Write, so no blanket must_not_modify applies; files_changed is always [] by the agent's own envelope.

codex-reviewer._note: INERT entry on a standard package install: broker.registry registers codex-reviewer ONLY when .claude/agents/codex-reviewer.md exists on the running machine (this-machine contrib-lane feature, never shipped in nexus-package/.claude/agents/) — see registry.py's _codex_lane_agent_files_present. This row exists only because test_deliverables_persona_drift.py asserts every DISPATCHABLE_PERSONAS member resolves here on a codex-lane-enabled machine. Out-of-family read-only judge seat, sandbox hard-pinned read-only; never reviews codex-worker's own output (cross-vendor rule: Codex never judges Codex).

fable-planner._note: INERT entry (DEC-035, Plexus meta-repo only). fable-planner is a Plexus-meta-only planning persona with NO nexus-package/.claude/agents/fable-planner.md and NO entry in this package's own dispatch-shape-guard.sh or router_core.py — it cannot actually be dispatched in an installed target. This row exists ONLY because nexus-broker/src/broker/registry.py is synced whole into this package by build_snapshot.sh, and test_deliverables_persona_drift.py asserts every DISPATCHABLE_PERSONAS member resolves to a contract here. must_not_modify mirrors the read-only personas (scout/lens) since there is no real package write-lane to describe.

planner._note: ACTIVE entry (DEC-074): planner is a real, gated dispatch target on an installed target — nexus-package/.claude/agents/planner.md is dispatchable:true (opus tier), and this package ships plan-validation-gate.py / gate_runner.py wiring / a dispatch-shape-guard.sh roster entry for it. Every return is scored by the independent plan-validation gate (SubagentStop, fail-closed) before it can land — planner is never its own judge. expected_paths/forbidden_paths mirror palette's write-lane shape (a narrow, non-source output surface).

forge-ui-pro._note: Retired dispatch NAME (R2-T03 FIX-4): escalation is a model-override (opus/xhigh) on forge-ui, never this name; persona-alias-resolver.sh redirects any dispatch to it before it ever reaches a gate, so it is retired from every gate roster (broker-gate/lens-gate/no-deferral-gate/skills-required-guard CODE_WRITING/GATED_AGENTS/FIXING_AGENTS sets) — kept here only as an inert historical record.

forge-wire-pro._note: Retired dispatch NAME (R2-T03 FIX-4): escalation is a model-override (opus/xhigh) on forge-wire, never this name; persona-alias-resolver.sh redirects any dispatch to it before it ever reaches a gate, so it is retired from every gate roster (broker-gate/lens-gate/no-deferral-gate/skills-required-guard CODE_WRITING/GATED_AGENTS/FIXING_AGENTS sets) — kept here only as an inert historical record.

forge._note: Tombstone fallback — pre-split persona key; live dispatches use forge-ui/forge-wire (escalation = model-override on the base persona).

pipeline._note: Tombstone fallback — pre-split persona key; live dispatches use pipeline-data/pipeline-async (escalation = model-override on the base persona).

quill._note: Tombstone fallback — pre-split persona key; live dispatches use quill-ts/quill-py.
